---
title: 'Greek gods and demi-gods'
date: '2012-01-21T09:38:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3811156501734062916'
blogger_filename: '/2012/01/greek-gods-and-demi-gods.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Studies'
  - 'Angels'
---

<br /><h1 style="background-color: white; border-bottom-color: rgb(204, 204, 204); border-bottom-style: dotted; border-bottom-width: 1px; color: #333333; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 20px; font-weight: normal; line-height: 20px; margin-bottom: 10px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 5px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px; text-decoration: none;">Greek gods and&nbsp;demi-gods</h1><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">The Fallen Angels are source of mystery and intrigue for many. They really aren’t that mysterious. God created Angels to&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/hebrews/passage.aspx?q=hebrews+1:9-14" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">serve Him by serving man</a>&nbsp;(<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/hebrews/passage.aspx?q=hebrews+1:9-14" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Hebrews 1:9-14</a>). When Lucifer fell from grace he took a third of the angels with him (<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/revelation/12-4.html" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Rev 12:4</a>). Some&nbsp;committed&nbsp;acts that got them locked up until judgement day and others roam the earth today.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Some have wondered what happened to the angels from&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/genesis/passage.aspx?q=genesis+6:4-7" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Genesis 6</a>&nbsp;that came to earth and had babies with the daughters of man. This created a race called&nbsp;Nephilim (or Giants) on the earth. Have you realized by now that all myths are based in some form of reality? Such is the case with these Greek Myths. “Greek gods” were nothing more than angelic beings who came to the race of men to interbreed. This created a true demi-god situation where men of extreme size and power were created. Some may have even been hideous.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">The&nbsp;Greek&nbsp;myths actually may give some insight into what those days were like, taken with a hefty dose of salt because they have been clouded in 1,000′s of years of story line and&nbsp;exaggeration.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">So if this happened once why not again? The purpose of the inbreeding can be found in the instructions given at the fall.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/genesis/passage.aspx?q=genesis+3:14-15" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Genesis 3:</a></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em>15 And I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your offspring and hers; he will crush your head, and you will strike his heel.”</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Satan/Lucifer knows that the offspring must be a pure bred human. So he begins a DNA manipulation plan to ruin the seed of mankind. This becomes so&nbsp;awful&nbsp;that there is only one human family left on earth that hasn’t been ruined for evil and God wipes the earth of the whole mess. He then locks up those angels for a judgement day to come.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/genesis/passage.aspx?q=genesis+6:4-8" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Genesis 6</a></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em>&nbsp;4&nbsp;<strong>The Nephilim were on the earth in those days–and also afterward–when the sons of God went to the daughters of men and had children by them.</strong>&nbsp;They were the heroes of old, men of renown. 5 The LORD saw how great man’s wickedness on the earth had become, and that every inclination of the thoughts of his heart was only evil all the time. 6 The LORD was grieved that he had made man on the earth, and his heart was filled with pain. 7 So the LORD said, “I will wipe mankind, whom I have created, from the face of the earth–men and animals, and creatures that move along the ground, and birds of the air–for I am grieved that I have made them.” 8 But Noah found favor in the eyes of the LORD.</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/jude/1-6.html" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Jude 6</a></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em>6&nbsp;<strong>And the angels who did not keep their proper domain, but left their own abode, He has reserved in everlasting chains under darkness for the judgment of the great day</strong>…</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/2-peter/2-4.html" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">2 Peter 2:4</a></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em>4&nbsp;<strong>For if God did not spare the angels who sinned, but cast them down to hell and delivered them into chains of darkness, to be reserved for judgment</strong>…</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-peter/passage.aspx?q=1-peter+3:18-20" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">1 Peter 3:18-20</a></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><em>18&nbsp;For Christ also suffered once for sins, the just for the unjust, that He might bring us&nbsp;<a href="" name="a" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;"></a>to God, being put to death in the flesh but made alive by the Spirit,&nbsp;19&nbsp;<strong>by whom also He went and preached to the spirits in prison,&nbsp;20&nbsp;who formerly were disobedient, when once the Divine longsuffering waited&nbsp;<a href="" name="b" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;"></a>in the days of Noah, while the ark was being prepared</strong>, in which a few, that is, eight souls, were saved through water.</em></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Whole mythologies were birthed from these events.&nbsp;Zeus and Poseidon and the whole mess were tales derived from these things.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">There are those who would say that angels cannot have babies because of Luke 20:</div><ul style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; list-style-type: square; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><li style="list-style-type: square; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 20px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong>Luke 20:31-41</strong></li></ul><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong>35</strong>&nbsp;But those who are counted worthy to attain that age, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry nor are given in marriage;&nbsp;<strong>36</strong>&nbsp;nor can they die anymore, for they are equal to the angels and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">One could say, however, that this is simply pointing out that angels do not die and is not a reference to their reproductive capabilities. They ate with&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/genesis/18.html" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Abraham while discussing Lot’s future in Sodom</a>. They made food for Elijah. They do not have corporeal bodies but they can interact well enough with ours that we don’t know if we entertained them or not. So they interact well enough with our form. It is likely true that they do not procreate among themselves and that their state and number if final. But we are not told that one way or the other in biblical&nbsp;precedent. &nbsp;Could Angels, being&nbsp;intelligent, use some form of genetic manipulation to have babies with mankind?</div><ul style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; list-style-type: square; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><li style="list-style-type: square; margin-bottom: 0px; margin-left: 20px; margin-right: 0px; margin-top: 0px; padding-bottom: 0px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong>Hebrews 13</strong></li></ul><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><strong>1</strong>&nbsp;Let brotherly love continue.&nbsp;<strong>2</strong>&nbsp;Do not forget to entertain strangers, for by so doing some have unwittingly entertained angels.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">It’s an interesting hypothesis. It certainly speaks to the Greek Mythologies basis in reality. There are those at the History Channel who would have you believe that the Greek Gods were Aliens who came down and interbred with Humans. That may be truer than THEY even realize, but it’s not Aliens from another world, but rather another dimension, the spirit realm.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">It’s an interesting Idea anyway… to be continued…</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;">Studies from others. Not&nbsp;necessarily&nbsp;endorsed but possibly useful.</div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.herealittletherealittle.net/index.cfm?page_name=Demons" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">http://www.herealittletherealittle.net/index.cfm?page_name=Demons</a></div><div style="background-color: white; color: #555555; font-family: Arial, Tahoma, Verdana; font-size: 12px; line-height: 20px; padding-bottom: 15px; padding-left: 0px; padding-right: 0px; padding-top: 0px;"><a href="http://www.history.com/" style="color: #008dcf; text-decoration: none;">http://www.history.com/</a></div>
